title: Inline-keyboard approve / adjust / reject over the existing disk-marker gate
description: Surface pending approval checkpoints in Telegram with an inline keyboard [Approve][Adjust][Reject]. Button callbacks write the same .approved.json / .rejected.json disk markers the existing approve/reject CLI writes (src/state/approval-state.ts), routing calendar plans and do-bridge promotions through the one existing gate. Adjust collects replacement text and writes an approved marker carrying it as editDelta (steer). Callbacks are whitelist-gated.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) compiles the approvals handler and inline-keyboard additions with zero TypeScript errors.
A unit test against a temp .bober/approvals dir shows tapping Approve writes <id>.approved.json matching the ApprovedMarker shape with approvedAt and approverId populated.

A unit test shows tapping Reject writes <id>.rejected.json carrying the supplied feedback in the RejectedMarker, and tapping Adjust then supplying text writes an approved marker whose editDelta equals that text.
A unit test shows a callback for a checkpoint id with no matching <id>.pending.json on disk writes no approval marker file (guard mirrors the approve CLI pendingExists check).
A unit test shows a button callback from a non-whitelisted user id writes no marker and triggers no resolution.
A pending calendar-plan checkpoint surfaces in Telegram with three buttons; tapping Approve resolves it so the waiting run proceeds.

nonGoals: Do not create a new approval mechanism — write the same .approved.json / .rejected.json markers used by src/state/approval-state.ts and the existing approve/reject CLI commands.
Do not auto-resolve checkpoints — every resolution requires an explicit button tap from a whitelisted user.
Do not write an approval marker when no matching .pending.json exists for the checkpoint id.
Do not bypass the sendSafe outbound funnel.
Do not modify the orchestrator or the approval-state schema — only read pending markers and write approved/rejected markers.
stopConditions: The approve, reject, adjust, missing-pending, and non-whitelisted-callback unit tests pass against a temp .bober/approvals directory and `npm run build` exits zero.
Each button tap writes exactly the marker file the matching approve/reject CLI action would write.
A callback from a non-whitelisted id writes nothing.
definitionOfDone: Pending approval checkpoints surface in Telegram as messages with an inline keyboard of Approve, Adjust, and Reject. Tapping Approve writes the <id>.approved.json marker; tapping Reject prompts for feedback and writes the <id>.rejected.json marker; tapping Adjust prompts for replacement text and writes an <id>.approved.json marker whose editDelta carries that text — the same disk markers the existing approve/reject CLI commands produce, so calendar plans and do-bridge promotions resolve through the one existing gate. Taps for a checkpoint with no pending marker, or from a non-whitelisted user, write nothing.
assumptions: Approval markers follow src/state/approval-state.ts:24-43 (PendingMarker / ApprovedMarker / RejectedMarker); the adapter imports savePending-style helpers directly per Q1.
Listing pending checkpoints uses listPending from src/state/approval-state.ts; the guard mirrors pendingExists used in src/cli/commands/approve.ts.
approverId is resolved from the Telegram sender id (the whitelist identity), analogous to resolveApprover in src/cli/commands/approve.ts.
outOfScope: Generating the checkpoints (calendar planner / do-bridge own that; this adapter only renders and resolves them).
A full approvals-browsing UI beyond listing pending items and acting on them.
generatorNotes: Add src/telegram/handlers/approvals.ts and src/telegram/keyboard.ts (inline keyboard builder). Import the disk-marker helpers directly from src/state/approval-state.ts (listPending, readPending, and the approved/rejected save helpers — read the module to confirm exact export names; PendingMarker/ApprovedMarker/RejectedMarker shapes are at src/state/approval-state.ts:24-43). A command (e.g. /pending) lists pending markers; each renders as a message + inline keyboard with callback_data encoding {action, checkpointId}. Wire a callback-query handler in bot.ts that: (1) re-checks the Sprint-1 whitelist on the callback's sender id; (2) for a missing pending marker, replies via sendSafe and writes nothing (mirror src/cli/commands/approve.ts pendingExists guard); (3) Approve -> write approved marker with approverId = sender id; Reject -> ask for feedback text, then write rejected marker; Adjust -> ask for replacement text, then write approved marker with editDelta = that text. Use node:fs/promises via the approval-state helpers. ESM .js imports, `import type`, no `any`.
evaluatorNotes: Use a temp project root with .bober/approvals containing a fixture .pending.json. Verify the three actions write byte-shaped-correct markers (compare against what approve/reject CLI writes). Verify the no-pending guard and the non-whitelisted-callback guard both write nothing. Verify replies go through sendSafe. Run build + the marker-writing unit tests.
